# Security Policy

## Supported versions

Treeze is early in development. Security fixes will generally target the latest version on the `main` branch unless a stable release process is introduced later.

## Reporting a vulnerability

Please do not open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.

Instead, report the issue privately to the maintainer.

Include as much detail as possible:

- Description of the vulnerability
- Steps to reproduce
- Affected files or components
- Potential impact
- Suggested fix, if known

## Scope

Security-sensitive areas may include:

- Websocket message handling
- Client/server protocol parsing
- Runtime session handling
- Static file serving
- User-provided widget data
- Browser event handling
- Future plugin or extension systems

## Expectations

Please give the maintainer reasonable time to investigate before publicly disclosing a vulnerability.

Treeze is currently a small early-stage project, so response times may vary.
